creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
Though this can, indeed, be overcome with large RAM and /tmp, the problem is aggravated by the build-parallelization. Setting MAKE_JOBS_UNSAFE=yes allows the build to proceed. But...

Even when I build the port with MAKE_JOBS_UNSAFE=yes, I still see multiple lto1-processes running -- using up all of my processors.

In fact, there are 8 processes -- which is the ostensible CPU-count here: 4 cores, doubled by the "hyper-threading". I see gmake running with -j8.

I also see these warnings repeated:
lto-wrapper: warning: jobserver is not available: '--jobserver-auth=' is not present in 'MAKEFLAGS'
lto-wrapper: note: see the '-flto' option documentation for more information

With the MAKE_JOBS_NUMBER is set to 5 here normally, during the regular -- parallelized -- build the load went up much higher than 8, and the /tmp filled up.

To summarize:
 . When building normally -- parallelized -- there are many lto1 processes. It appears, each parallel-running linking fires off $CPU_COUNT lto1-processes which both pointlessly compete for the CPU (the load far exceeds the CPU-count) and fill up /tmp
 . Running with MAKE_JOBS_UNSAFE=yes, there are 8 (processor-count) lto1-processes
 . The MAKE_JOBS_NUMBER-value is not respected

I think, the port ought to address these issues -- particularly, the first one.
